digest.top_line: INDIBLADE: 2y bladder-intact EFS 78%, OS 96% after induction ipi/nivo → chemoRT in cT2-4a N0-2 MIBC. Single-arm; IO's add-on over CRT alone unquantified.
digest.tldr: Bladder carried the day: INDIBLADE bolts dual checkpoint induction (ipi + nivo) onto trimodality preservation, hitting 2y bladder-intact EFS 78% and OS 96% in cT2-4a N0-2 MIBC. Single-arm with no chemoRT-alone comparator, so the IO increment over standard TMT stays unquantified; RT dose/fractionation not reported.

sites.intro: The day's only signal: dual IO induction layered onto trimodality bladder preservation, promising 2y numbers but single-arm.

studies.name: INDIBLADE
studies.tldr: 2y bladder-intact EFS 78% (0.67-0.9) and 2y OS 96% after induction ipi/nivo then chemoRT in cT2-4a N0-2 MIBC.
details: 🔍 Single-arm: induction ipilimumab + nivolumab → chemoradiation, bladder-preservation intent
🔍 Stage II/III MIBC, cT2-4a; nodes cN0-2 eligible (node-positive allowed)
💊 Dual checkpoint induction (ipi + nivo) layered onto trimodality bladder preservation
⚠️ RT dose, fractionation, target volume and concurrent chemo agent not reported in source
⚠️ Single-arm, 2y f/u; no randomised comparator vs chemoRT alone or radical cystectomy
⚠️ Bladder-intact EFS is a composite endpoint; IO's added contribution over CRT alone unquantified

verdict.rationale: Single-arm phase 2, 2y follow-up, no randomised comparator vs cystectomy or chemoRT alone; bladder-intact EFS a composite surrogate.
verdict.audience: Stage II/III MIBC (cT2-4a N0-2), bladder-preservation candidates

studies.significance: Node-positive disease was eligible (cN0-2), uncommon for a bladder-preservation cohort, yet 2y OS reached 96% (0.91-1). RT dose, fractionation, target volume and the concurrent chemo agent aren't in the source, so whether this transfers to your trimodality protocol is unconfirmed.
studies.monday_clinic: For a cT2-4a N0-2 MIBC pt weighing bladder preservation, this early single-arm signal puts induction ipi/nivo before chemoRT in scope; it says nothing about upfront-cystectomy candidates or N3/metastatic disease.

analysis_sections.label: Design
analysis_sections.body: **Single-arm phase 2**. Induction **ipilimumab + nivolumab** followed by chemoradiation, with 2-year follow-up. No randomised comparator arm.

analysis_sections.label: Population & inclusion
analysis_sections.body: Stage II/III muscle-invasive bladder cancer, **cT2-4a N0-2** (node-positive up to cN2 eligible). Bladder-preservation intent.

analysis_sections.label: Regimen
analysis_sections.body: Induction dual checkpoint blockade (**ipi + nivo**) then chemoradiation. IO dosing and concurrent chemo agent not stated in source.

analysis_sections.label: Radiotherapy
analysis_sections.body: Chemoradiation is the consolidative modality, but **RT dose, fractionation and target volume are not reported in source**, limiting transfer to a specific protocol.

analysis_sections.label: Applies to
analysis_sections.body: Represents cT2-4a N0-2 MIBC pursuing bladder preservation. Does not represent node-negative-only cohorts, N3/metastatic disease, or upfront-cystectomy candidates.

analysis_sections.label: Limitations
analysis_sections.body: Single-arm with no comparator; short 2-year follow-up; RT and chemo details absent from source; bladder-intact EFS is a composite endpoint.

open_questions: Randomised comparison vs chemoRT alone or cystectomy
Added benefit of induction ipi/nivo over chemoRT alone
Durability of bladder preservation beyond 2 years

related_trials.brief_title: Enfortumab Vedotin in Combination With Pembrolizumab vs. Concurrent Chemoradiotherapy (cCRT) in People With Muscle Invasive Bladder Cancer (EV-309)
related_trials.brief_summary: This study is being done to see how well two drugs (enfortumab vedotin and pembrolizumab) work together as a bladder preservation approach to treat patients with muscle invasive bladder cancer. The study will compare these drugs to concurrent chemoradiotherapy that is usually used to treat this cancer (standard of care). The study will enroll patients with muscle-invasive bladder cancer (MIBC) who have cancer that has not spread outside the bladder.
related_trials.eligibility_brief:
Inclusion Criteria:

* Has histologically confirmed initial diagnosis of muscle-invasive bladder cancer (MIBC) with predominant urothelial histology staged cT2-T4aN0M0
* Tissue comprising muscle-invasive urothelial cancer must be submitted for clinical staging at baseline
* Eligible for and agree…
related_trials.answers_question: Randomised comparison vs chemoRT alone or cystectomy
related_trials.relevance_phrase: phase 3 EV+pembro vs chemoRT bladder preservation

related_trials.brief_title: A Study of Mitomycin-c/ Capecitabine ChemoRadiotherapy Combined With Nivolumab Monotherapy or Ipilumimab and Nivolumab, as Bladder Sparing Curative Treatment for Muscle Invasive Bladder Cancer: the CRIMI Study
related_trials.brief_summary: A multicenter Phase 1b/2, two stage, open label study of MMC/Capecitabine ChRT combined with nivolumab monotherapy or nivolumab and ipilimumab combination therapy in adult (\>18 years) subjects with non-metastatic muscle invasive bladder cancer that qualify for ChRT with curative intent.
related_trials.eligibility_brief:
Inclusion Criteria:

* Be willing and able to provide written informed consent for the trial.
* Be ≥ 18 years of age on day of signing informed consent.
* Wish to preserve their bladder function or be ineligible for cystectomy.
* Must have undergone transurethral biopsy of the bladder tumor, within…
related_trials.answers_question: Added benefit of induction ipi/nivo over chemoRT alone
related_trials.relevance_phrase: chemoRT + nivo±ipi arms isolate ipi benefit

bookmarks.text:
‼️ INDIBLADE: stage II/III (cT2-4aN0-2) MIBC -> induction ipilimumab plus nivolumab -> CRT

2-year bladder-intact event-free survival is 78% (0.67−0.9) 🤩 

2-year overall survival was 96% (0.91−1)

Lots of bladders can be potentially spared!https://t.co/LzTe72GYHD
